children.label: a Notices of maximum and actual capacity requirements

children.label: A
children.text: may be based upon the type of equipment, type of service, number of subscribers, type or size or carrier, nature of service area, or any other measure; and1So in original. Probably should be “of”.1

children.label: B
children.text: shall identify, to the maximum extent practicable, the capacity required at specific geographic locations.

children.label: A
children.text: accommodating simultaneously the number of interceptions, pen registers, and trap and trace devices set forth in the notice under subsection (a)(1)(A); and

children.label: B
children.text: expanding to the maximum capacity set forth in the notice under subsection (a)(1)(B).

children.heading: Expansion to maximum capacity
children.label: 2 Expansion to maximum capacity
children.text: After the date described in paragraph (1), a telecommunications carrier shall, subject to subsection (e), ensure that it can accommodate expeditiously any increase in the actual number of communication interceptions, pen registers, and trap and trace devices that authorized agencies may seek to conduct and use, up to the maximum capacity requirement set forth in the notice under subsection (a)(1)(B).

children.heading: Notices of increased maximum capacity requirements
children.label: c Notices of increased maximum capacity requirements

children.heading: Notice
children.label: 1 Notice
children.text: The Attorney General shall periodically publish in the Federal Register, after notice and comment, notice of any necessary increases in the maximum capacity requirement set forth in the notice under subsection (a)(1)(B).

children.heading: Compliance
children.label: 2 Compliance
children.text: Within 3 years after notice of increased maximum capacity requirements is published under paragraph (1), or within such longer time period as the Attorney General may specify, a telecommunications carrier shall, subject to subsection (e), ensure that its systems are capable of expanding to the increased maximum capacity set forth in the notice.

children.heading: Carrier statement
children.label: d Carrier statement
children.text: Within 180 days after the publication by the Attorney General of a notice of capacity requirements pursuant to subsection (a) or (c), a telecommunications carrier shall submit to the Attorney General a statement identifying any of its systems or services that do not have the capacity to accommodate simultaneously the number of interceptions, pen registers, and trap and trace devices set forth in the notice under such subsection.

children.heading: Reimbursement required for compliance
children.label: e Reimbursement required for compliance
children.text: The Attorney General shall review the statements submitted under subsection (d) and may, subject to the availability of appropriations, agree to reimburse a telecommunications carrier for costs directly associated with modifications to attain such capacity requirement that are determined to be reasonable in accordance with . Until the Attorney General agrees to reimburse such carrier for such modification, such carrier shall be considered to be in compliance with the capacity notices under subsection (a) or (c).section 1008(e) of this title
